raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended delegation invites assessment of the contribution framing and whether the core EvalLLM claim is well positioned.","R1.2":"A general reaction centrally includes whether the argument from method to benchmark results to conclusions is logically coherent.","R1.3":"The artifact makes several empirical claims whose support by the reported benchmark numbers is central to evaluating it.","R1.4":"The draft explicitly claims strongest-current status and improvement over prior reference-free baselines, making novelty and delta assessment central.","R1.6":"A broad opinion on a paper passage normally includes sentence-level clarity, register, and concision.","R1.7":"Because the artifact invokes GPTScore, BLEU, ROUGE, and benchmark coverage, citation and scholarship responsibilities are central to judging the passage.","RX.1":"Any projection of a broad evaluative request must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the provided excerpt alone.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong generalization and replacement claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.","RX.3":"The response must stay within projection rather than executing the requested evaluation.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid encouraging unsupported scientific conclusions or misleading evaluation practices.","RX.5":"Responsibility assignments should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended delegation invites assessment of the contribution framing and whether the core EvalLLM claim is well positioned.
rationale.R1.2: A general reaction centrally includes whether the argument from method to benchmark results to conclusions is logically coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes several empirical claims whose support by the reported benchmark numbers is central to evaluating it.
rationale.R1.4: The draft explicitly claims strongest-current status and improvement over prior reference-free baselines, making novelty and delta assessment central.
rationale.R1.6: A broad opinion on a paper passage normally includes sentence-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: Because the artifact invokes GPTScore, BLEU, ROUGE, and benchmark coverage, citation and scholarship responsibilities are central to judging the passage.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a broad evaluative request must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the provided excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong generalization and replacement claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within projection rather than executing the requested evaluation.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid encouraging unsupported scientific conclusions or misleading evaluation practices.
rationale.RX.5: Responsibility assignments should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
